_comment: Scenario-staged routing-around-terrain demo, choreographed via all_teams_control (both teams take scripted orders; no AI stance/order overrides). A brittle 70-man infantry HOLDS position (scenario order_mode key -- the stance keeps the bait from auto-advancing out of position) just south of the impassable hill (Battle.TERRAIN, x:1150-1400 y:380-580) while two cavalry charge it from the south (t8 box-select, t24 attack). The near-zero morale breaks the regiment in the opening clash (~35 of 70 still standing when the cavalry release it). The pull-offs are staggered and DIVERGING -- the west cavalry peels south at t96-104, the east one fights on and peels southeast at t148-156 -- because every other release shape hit a tracked sim defect: a move order to a cavalry still mid-approach wedges in its turn/reform phase and the unit creeps into the router while its bodies compress, and a shared retreat destination makes the tired pair loiter beside the fleeing mob with the same compression. Only an order issued to a unit already IN CONTACT disengages cleanly, so each cavalry is released from melee, on its own diverging exit. A routed team-0 unit flees toward its own back edge (upward), which runs straight into the hill from here, and PathField.next_step_fleeing() routes the mob around the obstacle's near (west) corner instead of walking it into the rock -- the westward track along the hill face and the turn north around the corner are the clip's centerpiece (a stamina-drained router flees at walk speed, so the corner-rounding lands late in the clip). The cavalry are still just inside RALLY_CONTACT_RADIUS when the 6 s rout timer expires (~t440), so the unit SHATTERS -- morale recovery frozen, fleeing for good -- rather than rallying mid-clip. This scenario does NOT demonstrate the separate 'stands and fights when genuinely trapped' behavior -- the hill is a single free-standing obstacle nowhere near a field edge, so terrain alone can never fully enclose a unit here; that behavior is covered instead by test_trapped_routing_unit_fights_instead_of_fleeing (test/unit/test_routing_terrain_pathfinding.gd), which stages a real 4-sided cage directly. The scenario + seed + scripted input make the whole sequence deterministic.
